content:
Quick update — I've rebased rnull and rnvme to v7.0-rc3:

  • rnull-v7.0-rc3: 130 patches on v7.0-rc3
  • rnvme-v7.0-rc3: +13 patches on top of rnull

Only one conflict in blk_mq_prealloc_tag_set_tags (renamed from realloc + return type change in rc3). Everything else applied cleanly.
Branches: https://github.com/Engineer-HaMa/linux/tree/rnull-v7.0-rc3 / rnvme-v7.0-rc3
A message was moved here from #Block > Rebasing rnvme to v6.19 by SeungJong Ha.
SeungJong Ha has marked this topic as resolved.

Hi @SeungJong Ha ! I am currently rebasing on v6.19. I am doing some changes to pci/irq to support reconfiguration of the interrupt vectors. Allocating the vectors up front is not going to work for many devices. We need to be able to be able to bring up the admin queue, execute controller identify, set admin queue limits, and then set the queue count. If you allocate vectors up front, you risk allocating more vectors than the device supports.
You will see issues on systems that has more cores than IO queues.
SeungJong Ha has marked this topic as unresolved.

Hi @Andreas Hindborg , I'm using this driver for my research project.
It seemed to be rebased on a fairly old version (6.15), so I've been rebasing it to leverage the newest Rust abstractions for my project. If you're ready to share your 6.19-rebased rnvme, I'd be happy to exchange my rebase source from rnvme-v7.0-rc3.
My rebase not only contains research-purpose modifications, but also includes some patches that might be helpful for you (e.g., clippy fixes).
Thank you!

Thanks, I'll take a look if I can lift some of the clippy fixes. The rnvme code really could use some of that :grimacing:

I'm just about done with my rebase. I'll be sure to ping you when I publish the code.
Hi @SeungJong Ha . I'm probably not going to publish any more rebases of the rust nvme driver. See here for details.

Hi Andreas, thanks for the heads-up, and for maintaining rnvme all this time — it's been really valuable for my work. I read through the LKML thread and understand the decision. I'll keep maintaining my own branch independently for research purposes. Feel free to pick up any of my patches if they're ever useful!
SeungJong Ha has marked this topic as resolved.
